WP-CAP-001-mn94og8x.name: Setup Quality Assessment
WP-CAP-001-mn94og8x.description: Ability to assess the quality of a potential trade setup and assign a confidence tier (A+, B, C). This determines position sizing.
WP-CAP-001-mn94og8x.current_state: Hardcoded 5-item checklist in trade-approval. Items 1-2 are human-attestation free text. Items 3-5 pull from heatseeker data.
WP-CAP-001-mn94og8x.desired_state: Programmatic assessment using market data, chart structure, Heatseeker signals, VIX regime, and cross-ticker alignment. No human attestation required for data-available items.

WP-CAP-002-mn94og8x.name: Chart Structure Detection
WP-CAP-002-mn94og8x.description: Ability to identify key price patterns (double bottom/top, S&R levels, session levels) from historical candle data on multiple timeframes (hourly, 3-minute).
WP-CAP-002-mn94og8x.current_state: Not implemented. Market-data tracks live prices and session levels but has no historical candle storage or pattern detection.
WP-CAP-002-mn94og8x.desired_state: Historical candle storage (hourly + 3-min), swing point detection, double bottom/top pattern matching, S&R level identification with touch-count scoring.

WP-CAP-003-mn94og8x.name: Real-Time Market Data Feed
WP-CAP-003-mn94og8x.description: Live price data for ES, MES, NQ, MNQ with sub-second latency. Required for stop order monitoring, bracket management, and real-time risk tracking.
WP-CAP-003-mn94og8x.current_state: Simulated. Market-data service returns mock prices.
WP-CAP-003-mn94og8x.desired_state: Real market data feed (TopstepX API or third-party provider). Prices available via internal API for all trading services.

WP-CAP-004-mn94og8x.name: Broker API Integration
WP-CAP-004-mn94og8x.description: Ability to place, modify, and cancel orders through TopstepX API. Support for limit, market, stop, and bracket orders across multiple accounts.
WP-CAP-004-mn94og8x.current_state: Placeholder adapter in trade-execution. API endpoints documented but not implemented.
WP-CAP-004-mn94og8x.desired_state: Working TopstepX adapter with auth, order placement, position tracking, SignalR fill monitoring, and multi-account fan-out.

WP-CAP-005-mn94og8x.name: Economic Calendar Integration
WP-CAP-005-mn94og8x.description: Awareness of upcoming high-impact economic events for news blackout enforcement.
WP-CAP-005-mn94og8x.current_state: Manual list of event types in risk-management config. No actual calendar dates.
WP-CAP-005-mn94og8x.desired_state: Auto-populated economic calendar with dates/times for upcoming events. Risk management can query 'any events in next N minutes?' and get a real answer.

WP-CAP-006-mn94og8x.name: Trading Dashboard
WP-CAP-006-mn94og8x.description: Real-time visual dashboard for human oversight. Shows open positions, P&L, active signals, risk status, and system health.
WP-CAP-006-mn94og8x.current_state: No dashboard. Everything is curl commands to individual service APIs.
WP-CAP-006-mn94og8x.desired_state: Web-based dashboard accessible from any device. Real-time updates. Kill-switch button. Position management controls.

WP-CAP-007-mn94og8x.name: Backtesting Engine
WP-CAP-007-mn94og8x.description: Ability to test trading strategies against historical data before deploying them live.
WP-CAP-007-mn94og8x.desired_state: Feed historical candle + GEX/VEX data through the pipeline. Score strategy performance (win rate, avg R:R, max drawdown, consistency rule compliance).

WP-CAP-008-mn94og8x.name: Heatseeker Data Ingestion
WP-CAP-008-mn94og8x.description: Automated ingestion of GEX/VEX map data from Heatseeker source (currently manual screenshot review).
WP-CAP-008-mn94og8x.current_state: Manual. Human reviews Heatseeker maps and submits analysis via POST /analyze.
WP-CAP-008-mn94og8x.desired_state: Automated or semi-automated ingestion. Could be screen capture + OCR, API if available, or structured data feed.

data.mission: Semi-autonomous to fully autonomous trading of ES/MES and NQ/MNQ futures through prop firm accounts, using the Heatseeker GEX/VEX methodology as the primary signal source.

mvp.label: MVP — Semi-Autonomous, Human Approved
mvp.description: System generates trade proposals with confidence scoring and sizing recommendations. Human reviews and approves/rejects. System executes approved trades and manages risk.
success_criteria: Generate trade proposals from Heatseeker analysis with confidence score and sizing tier
Present proposals to human with enough context to make a fast yes/no decision
Execute approved trades with proper bracket orders (stop + target)
Enforce all prop firm rules automatically (drawdown, consistency, news blackout, no hedging)
Track P&L and daily exposure in real time

final.label: Final — Fully Autonomous
final.description: System identifies setups, scores them, sizes them, and executes without human approval. Human monitors via dashboard and can override or kill-switch.
success_criteria: Score setup quality and determine appropriate sizing
Execute trades without human gate when confidence exceeds threshold
Self-monitor for regime changes and adapt (news, VIX spikes, session transitions)
Provide real-time dashboard for human oversight
Support kill-switch for immediate position flattening

prop_firm_rules.description: All trading must comply with TopstepX prop firm rules at all times. Violation = account loss.
rules: Consistency rule (no single day > 30% of profit target)
No hedging (unidirectional only within correlated pairs)
Contract limits per instrument per stage
prop_firm_rules.enforcement: These are non-negotiable. The system must hard-block any action that would violate these rules. No override, no force flag.

risk_hierarchy.description: Order of priority when rules conflict.

constraints.sessions: Futures hours with Asia/London/NY session awareness. Off-hours: micros only.

risk-management.role: Risk checks and prop firm rule enforcement

trade-approval.role: Human gate and setup quality scoring

data.supply_economy_ask: The trading pipeline defines WHAT it needs (the capabilities above). The supply economy determines HOW to deliver each capability — which services research it, which services build it, how to test it, and how to deploy it. The Governor decomposes these requirements into work packages for supply services.
